subject: Properties of Expectation
topic: §46 Expected Value of a Function of Two Random Variables
question: A group of $N$ business executives throw their business cards into a jar. The cards are mixed, and each person randomly selects one. Find the expected number of people that select their own card.
explanation: Let $X$ = the number of people who select their own card. For $1 \le i \le N$ let $$X_i = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if the $i$th person chooses his own card} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$$ Then $E(X_i) = \Pr(X_i = 1) = \frac{1}{N}$ and hence $E(X) = \left(\frac{1}{N}\right)N = 1$
